parentQuestion: Food lost taste, dry eye, cannot close eye

scaffolds.q: CN VII carries taste from front 2/3 of tongue.
scaffolds.explain: If CN VII is damaged, taste from most of the tongue is lost.

scaffolds.q: Tears keep your eye moist and healthy.
scaffolds.explain: The lacrimal gland makes tears. CN VII controls this gland.

scaffolds.q: Closing your eye uses the orbicularis oculi muscle.
scaffolds.explain: This muscle circles the eye and squeezes it shut. CN VII controls it.

scaffolds.q: CN VII controls muscles of facial expression.
scaffolds.explain: Smiling, frowning, blinking, raising eyebrows - all CN VII.

scaffolds.q: A dry eye can become damaged without tears for protection.
scaffolds.explain: Without tears, the cornea can dry out and get scratched.

scaffolds.q: Multiple symptoms from one side suggest one nerve.
scaffolds.explain: When several functions fail together, look for the common nerve.

scaffolds.q: Bells palsy is a common condition affecting CN VII.
scaffolds.explain: Bells palsy causes sudden facial weakness, dry eye, and taste changes.

scaffolds.q: CN VII carries taste, controls tears, and moves facial muscles.
scaffolds.explain: The facial nerve has multiple functions all affected by damage.

scaffolds.q: Facial nerve palsy requires eye protection.
scaffolds.explain: Without eye closure, the cornea dries. Patients need artificial tears.
